# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build with Shell Commands

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      build_status: '#{ { "status" : "{0}" } }'

    inputs:
      build_command:
        description: 'Custom shell command to run during the build process'
        required: true

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up GitHub Actions for building and testing
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: '14'

    - name: Run custom build command
      run: |
        echo "Running build command ${{ inputs.build_command }}"
        ${{ inputs.build_command }}

    - name: Save build status
      id: save_status
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v2
      with:
        name: build-status
        path: .
        retention-days: 7

    - name: Use saved build status in other workflows
      use: actions/timeouts@v1
      with:
        minutes: 5
        timeout-key: ${{ steps.save_status.id }}_timeout

    - name: Checkout previous build artifact if available, otherwise checkout latest
      uses: actions/checkout@v2
      with:
        ref: '${{ steps.save_status.outputs.build_status }}'
